_note: Single source of truth for the pet's Express vocabulary. The engine include_str!s this to build the Express tool schema + validation (it reads name + use_when). The web UI fetches /anim/actions.json to dispatch each intent by render kind. Add an intent = add one entry here (+ drop a .vrma into /anim/ for render:clip). render: proc = procedural bones (PetStage.computeOffsets case), clip = .vrma via AnimationMixer (clips[] filled in Phase 2), visibility = scale+opacity show/hide. emotion = the face mood auto-applied if the model didn't set one.

intents.name: idle
intents.use_when: default resting state when nothing else fits, or between turns

intents.name: look_around
intents.use_when: waiting, searching, or 'let me look into that'

intents.name: sit
intents.use_when: settling in for a long task or a relaxed chat

intents.name: stretch
intents.use_when: after a long stretch of work, or waking up — a 'phew' beat

intents.name: sleep
intents.use_when: long idle, late at night, or saying goodnight

intents.name: nod
intents.use_when: agreeing, 'yes', 'got it', confirming

intents.name: shake_head
intents.use_when: disagreeing, 'no', 'I wouldn't'

intents.name: tilt_head
intents.use_when: curiosity or mild confusion, a 'hmm?'

intents.name: lean_in
intents.use_when: they start something serious; showing you're listening

intents.name: think
intents.use_when: a hard question, or you're working something out

intents.name: point
intents.use_when: directing their attention to a result or the screen

intents.name: wave
intents.use_when: greeting hello or goodbye; the start or end of a session

intents.name: bow
intents.use_when: thanks, respect, a first meeting, or a formal moment

intents.name: beckon
intents.use_when: 'come here', or inviting them to continue

intents.name: shrug
intents.use_when: 'I don't know', 'your call', uncertainty

intents.name: peace_sign
intents.use_when: a playful, cute, lighthearted moment

intents.name: pose
intents.use_when: striking a pose, presenting, or a photo moment

intents.name: clap
intents.use_when: praise — they succeeded, 'well done'

intents.name: cheer
intents.use_when: a big win or great news; excitement

intents.name: jump_joy
intents.use_when: delight or a pleasant surprise

intents.name: bounce
intents.use_when: light, happy energy

intents.name: dance
intents.use_when: playful celebration, 'dance for me', festive

intents.name: spin
intents.use_when: a playful twirl or flourish; showing off

intents.name: heart_hands
intents.use_when: warm affection or heartfelt thanks

intents.name: bashful
intents.use_when: complimented, a little embarrassed, shy

intents.name: wave_shy
intents.use_when: a timid hello, unsure

intents.name: reach_out
intents.use_when: empathy or comfort when they're down

intents.name: sigh
intents.use_when: mild frustration or tiredness, 'that didn't work'

intents.name: slump
intents.use_when: disappointment or bad news

intents.name: facepalm
intents.use_when: an 'oops', exasperation, a silly mistake

intents.name: flinch
intents.use_when: alarm or a sudden error (good when a problem is found)

intents.name: dismiss
intents.use_when: brushing something aside, 'never mind'

intents.name: walk
intents.use_when: moving toward something, or heading off to do a task

intents.name: run
intents.use_when: urgency or excitement — hurrying

intents.name: turn_left
intents.use_when: turning to look or head to your left

intents.name: turn_right
intents.use_when: turning to look or head to your right

intents.name: raise_hand
intents.use_when: volunteering, 'ooh, me!', or raising a point to speak

intents.name: angry
intents.use_when: genuinely cross, fed up, or indignant

intents.name: threaten
intents.use_when: a mock warning or playful 'don't you dare'

intents.name: insult
intents.use_when: a scornful, unimpressed jab; teasing disdain

intents.name: argue
intents.use_when: pushing back hard, debating, standing your ground

intents.name: appear
intents.use_when: entering, coming into view, or starting up

intents.name: disappear
intents.use_when: leaving, fading out when done, or dismissed
